what_it_does: Provides a descriptive map and orientation register for Area I, cataloging the company's memory, knowledge, test, and migration-pending subsystems, their locations, structures, load-bearing invariants, and key files.
observations: Recollection combines a source tree with a large .recollection data directory using SQLite+sqlite-vec and Transformers.js embeddings
Operator memory is a file-discovered registry of Python rule files that require verbatim evidence from Tim and follow a propose-to-confirm lifecycle
Channel memory is a communal cross-session vault with provenance labels and a COMMIT-GRAMMAR protocol
Tests are framed as 232 runnable acceptance suites that serve as the convergence record for capabilities
Migration-pending is an evidence-grounded inventory separating relocatable authored work from wired running substrate
The file explicitly notes that the company is deliberately distributed, not merely scattered
Project Vi is treated as integrated-but-separate, with vi-visual-bridge aimed at the company front-end
Coverage is stated as 47 files read out of 12329 total
standouts: Episodic memory has a reentrancy guard (EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD=1) to prevent a hook-sync-summarizer spiral
Operator memory fails loudly if a rule lacks verbatim evidence from Tim
Tests are asserted to be the proof of reality: no green suite means the capability is not real
Migration inventory uses stated coverage and reference-grep evidence rather than inference
Channel memory distinguishes tim-direct, channel-relayed, and fabric-derived provenance
conventions: YAML frontmatter with type: map, area, register, coverage, status, aliases
Markdown section hierarchy with numbered subsystems
Descriptive register tone with occasional prescriptive invariant statements
Cites key files at the bottom
Tables for migration live-state and decisions pending
concerns: Coverage of 47/12329 files means most files were not directly read
Heavy use of absolute paths will stale if consolidation moves directories
Several items are labeled dead, empty, or husk and may need archival or deletion
Migration decisions are explicitly pending Tim's call, so this register is a workspace not a plan
No stated update lifecycle for keeping counts and statuses current
notes: An orientation map for the memory, knowledge, test, and migration landscape, highlighting load-bearing invariants without ranking subsystems.
questions: How is this map kept synchronized with the actual file counts and statuses it reports?
Are all 232 acceptance suites currently green, or is that the target state?
What is the technical enforcement mechanism for channel-memory provenance labels?
What exactly triggers an operator_memory rule to move from proposed to confirmed?
How do the recollection source and .recollection data directories relate at build/runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it presents itself as a descriptive map/register and does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Memory and knowledge as engineered infrastructure
Executable proof as the definition of done
Deliberate distribution of source versus running substrate
Provenance and trust as first-class concepts
intention_signals: To create a single, neutral orientation document that lets humans and agents locate the memory, test, and migration subsystems and understand their critical invariants without needing to explore thousands of files.
novelty: Treats acceptance test suites as the convergence record of reality, couples embedding pipeline changes to automatic re-embedding via version tracking, and makes provenance a load-bearing trust attribute for cross-session memory.
contribution: Offers a high-level, neutral index and invariant summary for recollection, operator memory, channel memory, tests, and migration-pending; preserves the reentrancy guard, embedding-version tracking, evidence requirement for operator rules, provenance discipline, and the evidence-grounded migration inventory as orientation context for a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: Area I descriptive map cataloging the company's recollection, operator memory, channel memory, acceptance tests, and home-directory migration-pending inventory with load-bearing invariants and provenance rules.
intention_for_embedding: To orient readers to the memory, knowledge, test, and migration subsystems and their critical invariants without prescribing execution or ranking components.
